name: Canada
contextBlurb: Canada now has a federal stablecoin statute on the books, but the operational regime is still being built. Budget 2025 legislation enacted the Stablecoin Act through Bill C-15 on 26 March 2026. The new framework will place non-financial-institution fiat-backed stablecoin issuers under Bank of Canada supervision and require 1:1 high-quality liquid reserves, at-par redemption, governance, cybersecurity and recovery planning. Until the framework is fully brought into force, stablecoin activity in Canada still sits alongside the CSA's interim VRCA rules for trading platforms and existing AML oversight. Deposit insurance still does not cover cryptoassets or stablecoins.

legislation.title: Bill C-15 receives Royal Assent, enacting the Stablecoin Act as part of Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1.
legislation.summary: The federal Stablecoin Act creates a new framework for fiat-backed stablecoin issuance by non-financial institutions, to be supervised by the Bank of Canada, with reserve, redemption, governance and security requirements to be filled in through follow-on regulation.

legislation.title: Bill C-15 is introduced in Parliament to implement Budget 2025, including the new Stablecoin Act.
legislation.summary: Introduction of Bill C-15 moved Canada from discussion to legislation by proposing a dedicated federal statute for fiat-backed stablecoins, rather than relying only on securities-platform conditions and general payments law.

legislation.title: CSA decision document for Payward Canada restates interim conditions for trading value-referenced crypto assets (VRCAs).
legislation.summary: The Payward Canada decision confirms the CSA's interim approach: platforms may support VRCAs only if the token is fiat-referenced, redeemable at par, backed by segregated high-quality reserve assets and subject to disclosure and custodial safeguards.

news.headline: Bill C-15 (45-1) - Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Canada enacted the Stablecoin Act through Bill C-15 on 26 March 2026, but the regime is not yet fully in force. The Act sets up Bank of Canada supervision for non-financial-institution fiat-backed stablecoin issuers and points toward 1:1 reserve, par-redemption, governance and data-security rules. Until detailed regulations are completed and the framework comes into force, trading-platform access to stablecoins continues to rely heavily on the CSA's interim VRCA conditions.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Canada now has a dedicated federal Stablecoin Act for fiat-backed issuance by non-financial institutions, but trading-platform treatment still relies on the CSA's VRCA framework. In platform contexts, VRCAs are commonly treated as securities and/or derivatives, while the new federal framework focuses on payments-style issuer regulation under Bank of Canada supervision.
